```python
class Vertex:
    def __init__(self):
        self.inValues = defaultdict(set)  # Initialize everything to empty set
        self.outValues = defaultdict(set)  # empty set

    def calculateDataFlow(self, graph: Dict[Vertex, Set[Vertex]], eq: 'DataFlowEquations'):
        vertices = list(graph.keys())
        for v in vertices:
            self.inValues[v] = set()
            self.outValues[v] = set()

        isOK = True
        while isOK:
            isOK = False
            for v in vertices:
                out = self.inValues[v].copy()
                out.update(eq.generates(v))
                out.difference_update(eq.kills(v))

                if out != self.outValues[v]:
                    isOK = True
                    self.outValues[v] = out

                    for target in graph[v]:
                        self.inValues[target].update(out)
```

The `calculateDataFlow` method in the `Vertex` class corresponds to the original Java method. The `generates` and `kills` methods of the `DataFlowEquations` class are implemented as placeholders for your specific needs.
